Package org.eventb.core.pog

Class POGProcessorModule

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.core.tool.Module
org.eventb.core.pog.POGModule
org.eventb.core.pog.POGProcessorModule
All Implemented Interfaces:
IPOGProcessorModule, IModule, IProcessorModule
Direct Known Subclasses:
BaseModule, CommitHypothesesModule, FwdMachineRefinementModule, UtilityModule
public abstract class POGProcessorModule extends POGModule implements IPOGProcessorModule
Default implementation of a proof obligation generator processor module.
Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • IPOGProcessorModule
  • POGModule

  • Method Details

    • getFilterModules

      protected final IFilterModule[] getFilterModules()
      Overrides:
      getFilterModules in class Module

    • getProcessorModules

      protected final IProcessorModule[] getProcessorModules()
      Overrides:
      getProcessorModules in class Module

    • createPO

      protected final void createPO(IPORoot target, String name, IPOGNature nature, IPOPredicateSet globalHypotheses, List<IPOGPredicate> localHypotheses, IPOGPredicate goal, IPOGSource[] sources, IPOGHint[] hints, boolean accurate, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Creates a proof obligation in the specified file, if the goal is not trivial.
      Parameters:
      target - the target file
      name - the name of the proof obligation
      nature - the nature of the proof obligation
      globalHypotheses - the global hypotheses (shared between proof obligations)
      localHypotheses - the local hypotheses (not shared between proof obligations)
      goal - the goal to be proved
      sources - references to source elements from which the proof obligation was derived
      hints - hints for a theorem prover
      accurate - the accuracy of the PO sequent
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there has been any problem creating the proof obligation
      Since:
      1.3

    • acceptPO

      private boolean acceptPO(String name, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • putPredicate

      private void putPredicate(IPOPredicate predicate, IPOGPredicate pogPredicate, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • putPOGHints

      private void putPOGHints(IPOSequent sequent, IPOGHint[] hints, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • putPOGSources

      private void putPOGSources(IPOSequent sequent, IPOGSource[] sources, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

      private void putPOGPredicates(IPOPredicateSet hypothesis, List<IPOGPredicate> localHypothesis, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • traceModule

      private <M extends IModule> void traceModule(M module, String op, String kind)

    • initFilterModules

      private final void initFilterModules(POGProcessorModule pogModule, IPOGStateRepository repository, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Initialise filter modules in the order returned by getFilterModules().
      Parameters:
      repository - the state repository to pass to all modules
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem during the initialisation of one of the modules

    • endFilterModules

      private final void endFilterModules(POGProcessorModule pogModule, IPOGStateRepository repository, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • initProcessorModules

      protected final void initProcessorModules(IRodinElement element, IPOGStateRepository repository, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Initialise processor modules in the order returned by getProcessorModules().
      Parameters:
      repository - the state repository to pass to all processor modules
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem during the initialisation of one of the modules

    • processModules

      protected final void processModules(IRodinElement element, IPOGStateRepository repository, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Process an element using the child processor modules in the order returned by getProcessorModules().
      Parameters:
      element - the element to process
      repository - the state repository to pass to all processor modules
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem during processing

    • endProcessorModules

      protected final void endProcessorModules(IRodinElement element, IPOGStateRepository repository, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • initModule

      public void initModule(IRodinElement element, IPOGStateRepository repository, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Description copied from interface: IPOGProcessorModule
      Initialisation code for the module
      Specified by:
      initModule in interface IPOGProcessorModule
      Parameters:
      element - statically checked element
      repository - the state repository to use
      monitor - a progress monitor
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem initialising this module

    • endModule

      public void endModule(IRodinElement element, IPOGStateRepository repository, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Description copied from interface: IPOGProcessorModule
      Termination code for the module
      Specified by:
      endModule in interface IPOGProcessorModule
      Parameters:
      element - statically checked element
      repository - the state repository to use
      monitor - a progress monitor
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem terminating this module

    • makePredicate

      protected static final IPOGPredicate makePredicate(Predicate predicate, IRodinElement source)
      Creates a POG predicate with an associated source element reference to be stored in a PO file.
      Parameters:
      predicate - a predicate
      source - an associated source element
      See Also:
      • IPOPredicate

    • makeSource

      protected static final IPOGSource makeSource(String role, IRodinElement source) throws RodinDBException
      Creates a POG source with an associated source element reference to be stored in a PO file.
      Parameters:
      role - the role of the source
      source - the actual source element
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • IPOSource

    • makePredicateSelectionHint

      protected static final IPOGHint makePredicateSelectionHint(IPOPredicate predicate)
      Creates a predicate selection hint for predicate.
      Parameters:
      predicate - the predicate to select

    • makeIntervalSelectionHint

      protected static final IPOGHint makeIntervalSelectionHint(IPOPredicateSet start, IPOPredicateSet end)
      Creates an interval selection hint.
      Parameters:
      start - the predicate set immediately preceding the interval
      end - the last predicate set in the interval

    • getSequentHypothesis

      protected static final IPOPredicateSet getSequentHypothesis(IPORoot target, String sequentName)
      Each proof obligation has its own sequent hypothesis. This is an IPOPredicateSet associated with that sequent. There is only one per sequent.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Parameters:
      target - the target proof obligation file
      sequentName - the name of the sequent
      Returns:
      a handle to the predicate set that represents the hypothesis

    • makeNature

      protected static final IPOGNature makeNature(String description)
      Creates a POG Nature for description.
      Parameters:
      description - the description of the nature
      Returns:
      the unique POG Nature corresponding to the given description
      Since:
      1.3
      See Also:
      • IPOGNature

    • isTrivial

      protected static boolean isTrivial(Predicate pred)
      Returns whether the given predicate is in one of the following forms (which trivially hold):
      • ⊤
      • E ∈ Type
      • E ⊆ Type
      Parameters:
      pred - a typed predicate
      Returns:
      true if the given predicate trivially holds by typing
      Since:
      2.0

    • debugTraceTrivial

      protected void debugTraceTrivial(String poName)
      Tracing method to use when a trivial PO was filtered.
      Parameters:
      poName - the name of the current po that was filtered
      Since:
      2.0
